payload.answer: The required eval accounting fields are: token counts (input/output), provider/model IDs (for attribution), estimated price (cost based on usage and pricing), and latency percentiles (p50, p90, p95) to characterize response time performance.
payload.decision: Provide a clear explanation of the required evaluation accounting fields for model performance and cost tracking.

entity_resolution.reason: All entities are clearly defined in context; no disambiguation needed.

memory_cards.statement: Token counts measure input and output tokens used during model inference, essential for cost and usage tracking.
Provider and model IDs identify the specific service and model version used, enabling reproducibility and cost attribution.
Estimated price calculates the monetary cost based on token usage and provider pricing, critical for budgeting and ROI analysis.
Latency percentiles (p50, p90, p95) describe response time distribution, indicating performance under typical and high-load conditions.

details: No deprecated or ambiguous information included.
Response adheres to schema and constraints.
